name: mysql.uptime
definition: Seconds the MySQL server has been running since last start. Cumulative Sum — increases monotonically. Useful for detecting unexpected restarts (value resets to low).
absence_means: MySQL has not been scraped or is unreachable

name: mysql.threads
definition: Current thread counts by kind (cached/connected/created/running). Non-monotonic Sum — behaves like a gauge. kind=connected = active connections; kind=running = threads actively executing queries.
absence_means: MySQL has not been scraped; filter on kind attribute (connected|running|cached|created)

name: mysql.connection.count
definition: Total connection attempts since MySQL startup (successful + failed). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter — apply rate to get connections/s.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled in collector config (not a MySQL error)

name: mysql.connection.errors
definition: Connection error counts by type (auth failures, max_connections rejections, internal errors). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: Optional metric. Absence means not enabled — NOT absence of errors. connection_error attribute values: accept|auth|handshake|internal|max_connections|select|tcpwrap
cardinality_footguns: connection_error attribute adds cardinality — bounded enum (6-8 values)

name: mysql.max_used_connections
definition: Peak simultaneous connections since MySQL startup. Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Useful for capacity planning vs max_connections config.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.mysqlx_connections
definition: MySQL X-Plugin connection counts by status (accepted/errors/rejected). Default metric. Non-monotonic Sum. Only relevant when X-Plugin / Document Store is in use.
absence_means: Zero or absent = X-Plugin not in use (normal for most deployments). status attribute: accepted|errors|rejected

name: mysql.query.count
definition: Total queries executed (all statements including stored programs). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter — apply rate for QPS.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled in collector; cannot compute slow query ratio without it

name: mysql.query.client.count
definition: Total client-originated queries (excludes stored program sub-statements). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.query.slow.count
definition: Queries that exceeded long_query_time threshold. Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Key reliability signal — rising rate indicates workload degradation, missing indexes, or lock contention.
absence_means: Optional metric. Absence means not enabled OR long_query_time too high. Rate of 0 is healthy — even occasional slow queries matter at high QPS.

name: mysql.handlers
definition: InnoDB handler operation counts by kind. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Key insight: read_rnd_next = full table scans; read_key = index lookups; write = mutations. Rate of read_rnd_next vs read_key reveals index efficiency.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped or handler collection failed
cardinality_footguns: handler attribute has 20+ values — avoid grouping all in timeseries. Filter to: write|read_rnd_next|read_key|update|delete for operational dashboards.

name: mysql.row_operations
definition: InnoDB row-level operation counts by kind (deleted/inserted/reads/updated). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Measures actual data manipulation workload at the storage engine level.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped
cardinality_footguns: Safe — bounded kind enum (4 values: deleted|inserted|reads|updated)

name: mysql.commands
definition: MySQL command type counts (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/etc). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Breaks down workload by SQL command type.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled
cardinality_footguns: command attribute is bounded enum but has 50+ values — filter to top SQL types for dashboard

name: mysql.joins
definition: InnoDB join types by kind (full_join/full_range_join/range/range_check/scan). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Full joins and scans indicate missing indexes.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.sorts
definition: Sort operation counts by kind (merge_passes/range/rows/scan). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. merge_passes > 0 indicates sort buffer overflows — a leading indicator of sort performance issues.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped
cardinality_footguns: Safe — bounded sorts enum (4 values: merge_passes|range|rows|scan)

name: mysql.tmp_resources
definition: Temporary resource creation counts by kind (disk_tables/files/tables). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. High disk_tables/tables ratio = tmp_table_size undersized.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped
cardinality_footguns: Safe — bounded kind enum (3 values: disk_tables|files|tables)

name: mysql.table.io.wait.count
definition: Total table I/O wait events by operation (read/write/fetch) and table/schema. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Denominator for average wait time derived signal.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped or Performance Schema disabled. If absent, check performance_schema variable in MySQL.
cardinality_footguns: table_name + schema adds high cardinality — use top-list for table breakdowns

name: mysql.table.io.wait.time
definition: Total table I/O wait time in nanoseconds by operation and table/schema. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Numerator for average wait time. Primary latency signal for table-level I/O.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped or Performance Schema disabled
cardinality_footguns: table_name + schema high cardinality — use top-list for breakdowns

name: mysql.index.io.wait.count
definition: Total index I/O wait event counts by operation and index/table/schema. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped or Performance Schema disabled

name: mysql.index.io.wait.time
definition: Total index I/O wait time in nanoseconds. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Use with mysql.index.io.wait.count to derive average wait per index operation. Hot indexes indicate missing covering indexes or access pattern issues.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped or Performance Schema disabled

name: mysql.statement_event.count
definition: Per-digest statement execution counts. Optional metric — requires Performance Schema events_statements_summary_by_digest consumer. digest/digest_text attributes enable per-query-pattern analysis.
absence_means: Optional metric. Absence = Performance Schema statement digest consumer disabled or metric not enabled in collector
cardinality_footguns: digest_text very high cardinality — always use top-N limit

name: mysql.statement_event.wait.time
definition: Per-digest total query execution wait time in nanoseconds. Optional metric — requires Performance Schema. Numerator for per-digest average query latency.
absence_means: Optional metric. Absence = Performance Schema disabled or metric not enabled
cardinality_footguns: digest_text very high cardinality — always top-N

name: mysql.buffer_pool.limit
definition: Configured InnoDB buffer pool size in bytes. Non-monotonic Sum — behaves like a gauge. Use max aggregation with no post-function. This is the denominator for buffer pool utilization %.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.buffer_pool.usage
definition: Bytes used in InnoDB buffer pool by status (dirty/clean). Non-monotonic Sum — behaves like a gauge. Sum of dirty + clean = total in-use bytes. Divide by mysql.buffer_pool.limit for utilization %.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.buffer_pool.data_pages
definition: Number of InnoDB buffer pool data pages by status (dirty/clean). Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. High dirty page count = background flusher may be falling behind.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.buffer_pool.pages
definition: Buffer pool page counts by kind (data/dirty/free/misc). Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. free pages = headroom remaining; misc = administrative overhead.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.buffer_pool.operations
definition: InnoDB buffer pool operation counts by type. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Key values: read_requests = total logical reads; read_ahead = prefetched pages; wait_free = times waited for free buffer page (saturation signal).
absence_means: MySQL not scraped
cardinality_footguns: buffer_pool_operations has 10+ values — filter to: read_requests|read_ahead|wait_free for operational use

name: mysql.buffer_pool.page_flushes
definition: InnoDB buffer pool page flush requests. Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. High rate = dirty page flush pressure; can indicate write workload intensity or insufficient innodb_io_capacity.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.table_open_cache
definition: Table open cache status (open/miss/overflow/hit). Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. overflow > 0 means the table cache is undersized.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.locks
definition: Table lock acquisition counts by kind (immediate/waited). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. waited > 0 indicates table-level lock contention (DDL or MyISAM). For InnoDB row locks use mysql.row_locks instead.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.row_locks
definition: InnoDB row lock counts/time by kind (time/waits). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. kind=waits = lock contention events; kind=time = total ms waited (accumulated). Both are key InnoDB health signals.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped
cardinality_footguns: kind=time is in milliseconds total — rate gives ms/s (lock time accumulation rate), not current wait time

name: mysql.table.lock_wait.read.count
definition: Table-level read lock wait counts by schema/table and lock type. Optional metric. Requires Performance Schema table_lock_waits consumer.
absence_means: Optional metric. Absence = Performance Schema table lock consumer disabled or metric not enabled

name: mysql.table.lock_wait.read.time
definition: Total read lock wait time in nanoseconds by table/schema. Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: Optional metric

name: mysql.table.lock_wait.write.count
definition: Table-level write lock wait counts by schema/table. Optional metric.
absence_means: Optional metric

name: mysql.table.lock_wait.write.time
definition: Total write lock wait time in nanoseconds by table/schema. Optional metric.
absence_means: Optional metric

name: mysql.operations
definition: InnoDB data file I/O operations by kind (reads/writes/fsyncs). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. fsyncs rate reflects durability overhead — high with innodb_flush_log_at_trx_commit=1.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.page_operations
definition: InnoDB page operation counts by kind (created/read/written). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. read rate = physical I/O demand; written = checkpoint/flush workload.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.log_operations
definition: InnoDB redo log operation counts by kind (waits/write_requests/writes). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. waits > 0 = redo log too small or I/O bottleneck.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.double_writes
definition: InnoDB doublewrite buffer operation counts by kind (pages_written/writes). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Overhead of crash-safety mechanism. High rate = write-intensive workload.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.opened_resources
definition: Resources opened since startup by kind (file/socket/table). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. High table open rate = table_open_cache undersized.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.prepared_statements
definition: Prepared statement command counts by type (close/execute/fetch/reset/send_long_data). Default metric. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: MySQL not scraped

name: mysql.client.network.io
definition: Network bytes transferred between MySQL server and all clients by direction (received/sent). Optional metric. Cumulative monotonic counter. Use rate for bytes/s.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.table.rows
definition: Row count per table by schema. Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Useful for capacity planning and identifying large tables.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.table.size
definition: Table storage size in bytes by table/schema and type (data_length/index_length). Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.table.average_row_length
definition: Average row length per table in bytes. Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Wide rows contribute to large tables and higher I/O per row accessed.
absence_means: Optional metric — absence means not enabled

name: mysql.page_size
definition: InnoDB page size in bytes. Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Standard value is 16384 (16 KiB). Rarely changes.
absence_means: Optional metric

name: mysql.replica.sql_delay
definition: Configured replication delay in seconds (SET GLOBAL REPLICA_IO_DELAY). Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Zero = no intentional delay. Non-zero = delayed replication configured.
absence_means: Optional metric OR source node (replica metrics only appear on replicas)

name: mysql.replica.time_behind_source
definition: Seconds the replica SQL thread lags behind the source. Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Zero = caught up (with caveat: can show 0 on idle source even if genuinely behind). Growing value = replica cannot keep up.
absence_means: Optional metric OR source node (only emitted on replicas)

name: mysql.mysqlx_worker_threads
definition: X-Plugin worker thread pool counts by kind (available/active). Optional metric. Non-monotonic Sum — gauge-like. Only relevant when X-Plugin is in use.
absence_means: Optional metric OR X-Plugin not in use

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: MySQL is self-hosted — there is no direct billing metric. Proxy cost signals: mysql.table.size (storage cost) and mysql.operations/mysql.page_operations (I/O cost on cloud storage). Use storage growth trend and write amplification (page_operations vs row_operations) for cost attribution.
absence_means: N/A
